riddle:
Here is the revised riddle with the specified changes:

1. Seek the page for a 19th-century figure known for his fight against slavery; from its opening line tell me how old he was when he died, and then follow the link that discusses legal acts freeing enslaved people to continue.

2. There exists an article about the practice of freeing enslaved people — report how many languages are shown in its interlanguage links, and next look up the Wikipedia entry for the congressional act that admitted California to the Union.

3. What integer results when you convert 21°C into its Fahrenheit-scale offset (multiply by 1.8 and take the whole-number part), and then follow the link on that act's page to the article about Mexican-era land grants in California.

4. If you land on the page about those historic California land grants, convert 21 metres into feet (take the integer part) before moving on to the page that describes California's upper legislative chamber.

5. The article whose opening line names that upper chamber will state how many seats it contains; record that number, and then click the linked "President" entry to open the page for the state's deputy executive.

6. In the deputy executive's article you will find an "incumbent since" date — note the date on which the current holder was sworn in, after which search for the Wikipedia page about the chief executive of Arkansas.

7. The helmsman of Arkansas keeps a date marking the creation of the captain's post; on the governor's page find the date the office was first formed, then consult the overview page for Louisiana's political system.

8. (On the page surveying Louisiana's politics) identify the name of the state's governor and follow the inline link labeled "Article" to the governor's main article for confirmation.

9. Now count how many vowels appear in that governor's full name; that vowel count is your next numeric clue.

10. Which body functions as Louisiana's upper legislative chamber, and how many members sit in it according to its article; after noting the number, click the linked "Article" to reach that chamber's dedicated page.

11. Reverse the digits of the number you just extracted to create a new integer (for example, 12 becomes 21); that reversed figure is the value you will use next.

12. Locate the municipal page for the city of Maitland (the page maintained by its local council) and report the total area of the city in square kilometres as listed there.

13. The area figure you obtained should be reduced to a single digit by repeatedly summing its digits until one digit remains; give that digital root.

14. Finally, compute the absolute difference between clue 3 and clue 11, then take that result modulo 10 (i.e., compute abs(clue 3 - clue 11) % 10); your final answer must be a single digit between 0 and 9.

metadata.theme: From individual abolitionism to laws, land grants and state institutions: tracing how personal emancipation (manumission) and early national governance shaped state formation and land politics in 18th–19th century America
